text: An 'extra-terrestrial relay' — a satellite in geostationary orbit — would allow radio and television signals to be broadcast to the entire world.
author: Arthur C. Clarke

didItHoldUp.analysis: Clarke described the geostationary communication satellite 12 years before Sputnik and 17 years before Telstar. He specified the exact orbital altitude (35,786 km), the concept of geostationary positioning, and the global communication capability. The geostationary orbit is sometimes called the 'Clarke Orbit' in his honor. Today, hundreds of satellites occupy this belt, doing exactly what he envisioned. Clarke is the rare case of a science fiction author receiving credit as a genuine inventor — of the idea itself.
